title: [Bug]: release GitHub auth helper should not fail when quiet auth status is unreliable
summary: The local release authentication helper can report GitHub CLI authentication as invalid when the quiet `gh auth status` path returns non-zero even though authenticated GitHub API calls succeed.
observed: During release preparation, direct GitHub CLI operations and authenticated API checks succeeded, while `scripts/check-gh-auth.sh --check-only` failed because it relied only on a quiet `gh auth status` invocation.
expected: The release helper should validate the capability needed by release operators: making authenticated GitHub API requests without printing token values. A noisy or unreliable status-only check should not block release preparation when API authentication is usable.
reproduction: Use a fake `gh` executable where `gh auth status` returns non-zero and `gh api --hostname github.com user --silent` returns zero, then run `scripts/check-gh-auth.sh --check-only`.
failing_test: Add a shell-helper regression test proving `scripts/check-gh-auth.sh --check-only` succeeds when an authenticated API probe succeeds after a status-only failure.
impact: Release preparation can be blocked by a local helper false negative. Runtime sink delivery semantics are unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to local maintainer release automation and cannot affect JetStream ACK, retry, DLQ, or sink commit behavior.
security: The helper must continue to avoid printing token values, credentials, private endpoints, or sensitive repository metadata. The regression uses a fake GitHub CLI and does not require live credentials.
acceptance: The helper accepts an authenticated API probe as proof that local GitHub CLI authentication is usable.
The helper still fails closed when both the status check and authenticated API probe fail.
The helper does not print token values or other credential material.
A deterministic fake-gh regression covers the false-negative status path.
The latest test report and changelog record the release-preparation fix.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_check_gh_auth_script.py -q, scripts/check-gh-auth.sh --check-only, and the focused release-preparation documentation checks.
documentation: Update the latest test report and changelog. Public release documentation does not need a new operator step unless the command behavior changes.
closeout: Close after the fix merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
